title: phanera roxburghiana
intro: Phanera roxburghiana, also known as the Roxburgh's Ficus or Tiger's Claw, is a species of plant in the family Moraceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, offering benefits such as pain relief, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, Phanera roxburghiana is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or ointments, allowing for the extraction and utilization of its active compounds.
